log_entries.thought_preview: Sample overview confirms it's a PE32 executable (126KB) with runtime modules and an overlay. Now I need to get all IOC types to understand the full scope of available data (IPs, domains, files, regist
IOC types show no network activity (0 IPs, domains, HTTP), 1 registry persistence key, 4 dropped files, and 0 processes created (likely due to sandbox limitations). I need to investigate the registry

The registry change shows a persistence mechanism: it modifies HKCR\exefile\shell\open\command to execute C:\Windows\svchost.com "%1" %*. This is a classic technique to associate a malicious file with

The dropped files confirm the malware drops svchost.com (both in Temp and C:\Windows) with the same SHA256 hash. This is the payload that will be executed via the registry hook. I need to check proces
Process activity shows no child processes created (likely sandbox limitation), but notable modules include Adobe Reader and various system DLLs. The mutex creation wasn't captured in process activity

Network activity confirms no C2 communication (0 IPs, domains, HTTP). The malware appears to be a fileless or self-contained dropper that modifies the registry to associate .exe files with a malicious

I have gathered all available data. The malware is a Delphi-compiled PE32 executable that uses XOR encoding and drops a malicious file named 'svchost.com' to C:\Windows and Temp directories. It create
